steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces a second anti-counterfeiting action (stamping sage-leaf imprints) after the first sentence already listed two steps (weaving blue threads and muscovite into the paper). Determine what logical relationship the blank must express between these two sentences.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Moreover,': ‘Moreover’ signals an additional item on top of what was already mentioned. Stamping sage-leaf imprints is a separate, parallel step alongside weaving threads and muscovite—this transition correctly adds another anti-counterfeiting measure. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Specifically,': ‘Specifically’ narrows or zooms in on the previous statement. But the sage-leaf imprinting is not a specific detail of weaving threads and muscovite—it is a different technique entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — 'For example,': ‘For example’ introduces an illustration of what was just said. The sage-leaf imprinting does not illustrate weaving threads and muscovite; it is an independent step, not an instance of the prior technique. ✗
  • Choice D — 'That said,': ‘That said’ signals a contrast or concession. Nothing in the second sentence contradicts or qualifies the first—both sentences describe anti-counterfeiting measures Franklin took. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
